title: Enterovirus (EV) A71 real-time RT-PCR (EV-A71-TM2018)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"g77d","text":"This protocol aims to amplify enterovirus (EV) A71 viruses but not other viruses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"573ko","text":"This protocol was designed by us.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ohne","text":"The oligonucleotides target the 5'UTR noncoding region. This is a qualitative test for investigating EV-A71 infection of humans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqul5","text":"The test has identified both historical EV-A71 strains and contemporary strains and has confirmed these using subgenomic sequencing of partial 5'UTR-VP2 and partial VP1 sequencing. Culture of the virus is not required as this assay is capable of detecting EV-A71 direct from extracted clinical samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":135,"length":17}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C5FFE4C0F29911E8864FE1A71DF87CC6","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.nz7df9n"}}}}
steps: %s %s The design philosophies. to ensure our assay detects old and new variants of EV-D68 to reduce the total number of degenerate positions in any single primer
SuperScript™ III Platinum™ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit Life Technologies Catalog #11732088

The assay has been used on both a Rotor-Gene 6000 real-time thermocycler Prepare sufficient mix for the number of reactions. Include a suitable 'dead volume' as necessary if using a robotic dispenser. %s %s Dispense 15µL to each reaction vessel. Add 5µL of template (extracted RNA, controls or no-template control [NTC; nuclease-free water] ). Total reaction volume is 20µL

The definition used for a satisfactory positive result from a real-time fluorogenic PCR should include each of the following: A sigmoidal curve – the trace travels horizontally, curves upward, continues in an exponential rise and followed by a curve towards a horizontal plateau phase A suitable level of fluorescence intensity as measured in comparison to a positive control (y-axis) A defined threshold (C T ) value which the fluorescent curve has clearly exceeded (Fig.1 arrow), which sits early in the log-linear phase and is <40 cycles A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or a curve that crosses the threshold with a C T >40 cycles is considered a negative result. NTCs should not produce a curve a Figure 1 . Examples of satisfactory sigmoidal amplification curve shape when considering an assay’s fluorescent signal output. The crossing point or threshold cycle (C T ) is indicated (yellow arrow); it is the value at which fluorescence levels surpass a predefined (usually set during validation, or arbitrary) threshold level as shown in this normalized linear scale depiction. LP-log-linear phase of signal generated during the exponential part of the PCR amplification; TP-a slowing of the amplification and accompanying fluorescence signal marks the transition phase; PP-the plateau phase is reached when there is little or no increase in fluorescent signal despite continued cycling.
creator: Ian M Mackay
